verdict_reasoning: LAMP1 is a lysosomal membrane glycoprotein that is constitutively resident on lysosomal membranes but transiently reaches the plasma membrane outer face during lysosomal exocytosis. This occurs in activated T cells, NK cells, and other lymphocytes during degranulation and immune activation. Its CD107a designation reflects this surface exposure as a cell-activation marker, and it is clinically engaged in CAR-T and TCR-T monitoring via flow cytometry. Surface accessibility is cell-state-dependent and well-documented.
